name: Port Royal - Sin, Shame and Sea (v1.1)
description:
Step into the sun-bleached, vice-ridden streets of Port Royal, 1685, where a gold piece and a sharp blade are the only true laws. In this open-ended sandbox of piracy, trade, and brutal survival, you carve your own legend from the flesh of the living and the bones of the dead.

This RPG is a slow-burn, open-ended sandbox where you are the author of your own brutal saga. You will navigate the complex social hierarchy of this pirate haven, from the filthy docks where salt-wet slaves are auctioned off, to the gilded parlours where deals are struck over fine wine and forged documents. Your choices will ripple through the town’s fabric, shaping alliances and enmities with a diverse cast of NPCs—from weary captains and cunning prostitutes to brutal slave traders and corrupt officials. The world is unforgiving, violence is intimate and graphic, and every encounter is an opportunity for gain, or a step closer to the gibbet cage. In Port Royal, fame is fleeting, but infamy lasts forever.

I've put a lot of work to build a realistic impression of Port Royal at the end of the 17th century and of its society. Various openings allow you to explore the town from different points of view: a sea captain, an adventurer, a sailor, a freshly disembarked slave, a prostitute, etc.

I was working on a new character (Red Ruth - Pirate, Harlot, Thief), and I needed a lore book about Port Royal in the golden age of the Caribbean privateers. I spent a lot of time on it, on the period, and on transcribing the ancient maps of the town into a system of map indices. Finally, I decided to release it in advance as an RPG world. I can now go back to developing the character.

Version history:
- 1.1: New opening: Isekai. You've been teleported to Port Royal from the modern world. You are invited to choose your clothes but you can also leave it random, or give more details about your starting situation.
